3.name: Describe Security, Compliance, Privacy, and Trust

4.name: Describe Microsoft 365 Pricing, Licensing, and Support

questions.question: Which cloud deployment model makes computing resources available to the general public over the internet?
questions.explanation: A public cloud makes computing resources available to the general public over the internet. The infrastructure is owned and managed by a third-party cloud provider like Microsoft Azure.

questions.question: Which cloud deployment model is dedicated to a single organization and can be hosted on-premises or by a third party?
questions.explanation: A private cloud is dedicated exclusively to one organization. It can be hosted on-premises in the organization's data center or by a third-party provider, offering greater control and privacy.

questions.question: Which cloud model combines on-premises infrastructure with public cloud services?
questions.explanation: A hybrid cloud combines on-premises infrastructure (or private cloud) with public cloud services, allowing data and applications to move between the two environments.

questions.question: Microsoft 365 is an example of which cloud service model?
questions.explanation: Microsoft 365 is a SaaS (Software as a Service) offering. Users access fully managed applications like Outlook, Teams, and Word through the internet without managing infrastructure.

questions.question: Which cloud service model provides virtual machines, storage, and networking resources?
questions.explanation: IaaS (Infrastructure as a Service) provides virtualized computing resources including virtual machines, storage, and networking. The customer manages the OS, middleware, and applications.

questions.question: Which cloud service model provides a platform for developers to build, test, and deploy applications without managing underlying infrastructure?
questions.explanation: PaaS (Platform as a Service) provides a platform with tools, services, and environments for developers to build, test, and deploy applications without managing servers, storage, or networking.

questions.question: Which cloud benefit refers to the ability to increase or decrease resources based on demand?
questions.explanation: Scalability is the ability to increase or decrease computing resources (compute, storage, network) based on demand. Cloud services can scale up/down (vertical) or out/in (horizontal).

questions.question: Which cloud benefit ensures that services remain available with minimal downtime?
questions.explanation: High availability ensures that cloud services remain operational with minimal downtime. Cloud providers achieve this through redundant infrastructure, failover mechanisms, and SLAs.

questions.question: Which cloud benefit allows an organization to automatically scale resources to match sudden spikes in demand?
questions.explanation: Elasticity is the ability to automatically or dynamically scale resources up or down to match demand. Unlike scalability (which is the capability), elasticity is the automatic adjustment.

questions.question: Moving from on-premises infrastructure to cloud services shifts spending from which type of expenditure to which other type?
questions.explanation: Cloud computing shifts spending from CapEx (buying and maintaining physical hardware) to OpEx (paying for services on a consumption-based model). This reduces upfront costs.

questions.question: In the shared responsibility model, who is responsible for securing the physical infrastructure of a cloud data center?
questions.explanation: In the shared responsibility model, the cloud provider is always responsible for securing the physical infrastructure, including data centers, hardware, and the network backbone.

questions.question: In the shared responsibility model for SaaS, who is responsible for user identity and access management?
options: The customer, with tools provided by the cloud provider
questions.explanation: Even in SaaS, the customer is responsible for managing user identities, access controls, and authentication policies. The cloud provider supplies the tools (like Azure AD) but the customer configures them.

questions.question: In the shared responsibility model, which layer shifts the MOST responsibility to the customer?
questions.explanation: IaaS shifts the most responsibility to the customer. The customer manages the OS, middleware, runtime, data, and applications. The provider only manages the physical infrastructure and virtualization.

questions.question: An organization wants to keep sensitive patient data on-premises but use the public cloud for non-sensitive workloads. Which deployment model is best?
questions.explanation: A hybrid cloud allows the organization to keep sensitive data on-premises (private cloud) while leveraging the public cloud for non-sensitive workloads, optimizing cost and compliance.

questions.question: What does the pay-as-you-go pricing model in cloud computing mean?
options: You pay a fixed monthly fee regardless of usage
You only pay for the resources you consume
You prepay for five years of service
You pay a one-time licensing fee
questions.explanation: Pay-as-you-go (consumption-based) pricing means you only pay for the cloud resources you actually use. When you stop using resources, you stop paying for them.

questions.question: Which cloud benefit allows an organization to deploy resources in data centers around the world to serve users with low latency?
questions.explanation: Geo-distribution allows organizations to deploy resources in multiple cloud regions worldwide, reducing latency for users by serving them from the nearest data center location.

questions.question: Which cloud service model requires the customer to manage the operating system and applications running on virtual machines?
questions.explanation: In IaaS, the customer manages the operating system, middleware, runtime, applications, and data. The cloud provider manages only the physical infrastructure and virtualization layer.

questions.question: Which cloud benefit refers to the ability to quickly deploy new resources and services in minutes?
questions.explanation: Agility refers to the ability to rapidly provision and deploy cloud resources. Organizations can set up new environments, test ideas, and launch services in minutes instead of weeks.

questions.question: In a SaaS model like Microsoft 365, which of the following is the customer's responsibility?
options: Configuring user accounts and access policies
questions.explanation: In SaaS, the customer is responsible for their data, user accounts, access policies, and device management. The cloud provider handles everything else including application patching and infrastructure.

questions.question: Which cloud deployment model provides the greatest control over security and compliance but requires the most capital investment?
questions.explanation: A private cloud provides the greatest control over security, compliance, and customization since the organization owns or leases the infrastructure. However, it requires significant CapEx investment.

questions.question: Which cloud benefit ensures that data is protected and recoverable in the event of a disaster?
questions.explanation: Disaster recovery ensures that data and applications can be recovered after a major outage or disaster. Cloud providers offer built-in backup, replication, and recovery capabilities.

questions.question: A company uses Azure Virtual Machines (IaaS) for development, Azure App Service (PaaS) for web hosting, and Microsoft 365 (SaaS) for productivity. In which service does the company have the LEAST management responsibility?
questions.explanation: Microsoft 365 (SaaS) requires the least management responsibility. The provider manages everything from infrastructure to application updates. The customer only manages users, data, and access.

questions.question: In the shared responsibility model, who is always responsible for protecting their data and managing who has access to it, regardless of the cloud service model?
questions.explanation: Regardless of the service model (IaaS, PaaS, or SaaS), the customer is always responsible for their data, user accounts, identities, and access management.

questions.question: An organization's existing on-premises servers cost $500,000 to replace every 5 years (CapEx). Moving to the cloud would cost $10,000/month (OpEx). What is a key financial advantage of the cloud model?
options: The total 5-year cost is always lower in the cloud
No upfront capital investment is needed, and costs align with actual usage
Cloud costs are always fixed and predictable
The organization avoids all IT costs
questions.explanation: The key advantage is eliminating large upfront CapEx and replacing it with predictable monthly OpEx that can scale with usage. This improves cash flow even if total costs are similar.

questions.question: An organization uses AWS for some workloads and Azure for others. What is this deployment strategy called?
questions.explanation: Multi-cloud refers to using services from multiple public cloud providers (e.g., AWS and Azure). This differs from hybrid cloud, which combines on-premises/private cloud with public cloud.

questions.question: Gmail, Salesforce, and Microsoft 365 are all examples of which cloud service model?
questions.explanation: Gmail, Salesforce, and Microsoft 365 are all SaaS applications. Users access them through a web browser without managing any underlying infrastructure or platform components.

questions.question: In the shared responsibility model, which responsibility always belongs to the cloud provider across all service models?
questions.explanation: Physical security of data centers is always the cloud provider's responsibility, regardless of whether the service model is IaaS, PaaS, or SaaS.

questions.question: Which term describes the cloud's ability to provide consistent performance and uptime as guaranteed by a service agreement?
questions.explanation: Reliability refers to the cloud's ability to provide consistent performance and uptime. Cloud providers back this with Service Level Agreements (SLAs) that guarantee specific uptime percentages.

questions.question: A developer wants to deploy a web application without worrying about server provisioning, OS updates, or load balancing. Which cloud model is most appropriate?
options: IaaS — deploy to a virtual machine
PaaS — deploy to a managed app service
On-premises — deploy to a physical server
questions.explanation: PaaS is ideal for developers who want to focus on their application code. The platform handles server provisioning, OS updates, scaling, and load balancing automatically.

questions.question: Which cloud computing benefit reduces the risk of hardware failure affecting business operations?
questions.explanation: Fault tolerance is the cloud's ability to continue operating even when one or more components fail. Redundant systems ensure that hardware failures do not affect business operations.

questions.question: When using PaaS, which of the following is the cloud provider's responsibility but NOT the customer's?
questions.explanation: In PaaS, the cloud provider manages the OS, including patching and updates. The customer is responsible for application code, data, and user access management.

questions.question: Which cloud deployment is best for an organization that needs to meet strict data residency regulations requiring data to remain in a specific country?
options: Private cloud or public cloud with specific region selection
Hybrid cloud with no on-premises component
questions.explanation: A private cloud or a public cloud deployment in a specific region ensures data stays within the required country or jurisdiction, meeting data residency and sovereignty regulations.

questions.question: An organization needs to handle a sudden 10x spike in web traffic during a product launch without pre-provisioning resources. Which two cloud benefits address this?
questions.explanation: Elasticity automatically scales resources to handle the spike, while scalability ensures the cloud can accommodate growth. Together, they handle sudden demand increases without pre-provisioning.

questions.question: Which cloud service model gives the customer the most control over the computing environment?
questions.explanation: IaaS gives the customer the most control. The customer manages the OS, middleware, runtime, data, and applications while the provider manages only the physical infrastructure.

questions.question: According to the shared responsibility model, endpoint protection (securing user devices) is whose responsibility?
questions.explanation: Endpoint protection — securing user devices like laptops, phones, and tablets — is always the customer's responsibility, regardless of the cloud service model being used.

questions.question: A retail company runs an e-commerce site in the public cloud but processes payments through a private on-premises system to meet PCI DSS requirements. This is an example of which model?
questions.explanation: Using a public cloud for the e-commerce frontend and a private on-premises system for payment processing is a hybrid cloud model, combining public and private environments for different workloads.

questions.question: Which cloud benefit means that cloud providers handle hardware maintenance, software updates, and infrastructure management?
questions.explanation: Cloud computing reduces management overhead by shifting infrastructure maintenance, hardware replacement, and software updates to the cloud provider, freeing the customer to focus on business.

questions.question: In which cloud service model does the shared responsibility boundary place the MOST security responsibilities on the cloud provider?
options: All models have identical provider responsibilities
questions.explanation: In SaaS, the cloud provider takes on the most responsibility — managing the application, runtime, middleware, OS, and infrastructure. The customer primarily manages identities, data, and devices.

questions.question: Which Microsoft 365 application provides a hub for teamwork with chat, meetings, and file collaboration?
questions.explanation: Microsoft Teams is the hub for teamwork in Microsoft 365, providing persistent chat, video meetings, file sharing, and integration with other Microsoft 365 apps and third-party services.

questions.question: Which Microsoft 365 service provides team sites and intranet portals for content management and collaboration?
questions.explanation: SharePoint Online provides team sites, intranet portals, document libraries, and content management capabilities for organizations. It enables collaboration through shared workspaces.

questions.question: Which Microsoft 365 service provides personal cloud storage for individual users?
questions.explanation: OneDrive for Business provides each user with personal cloud storage for their files. Files can be synced across devices and shared with others within or outside the organization.

questions.question: Which Microsoft 365 service provides enterprise email, calendaring, and contacts?
questions.explanation: Exchange Online is the cloud-based email service in Microsoft 365, providing enterprise email, calendar, contacts, and tasks with built-in spam filtering and compliance features.

questions.question: What is the primary email client application in Microsoft 365?
questions.explanation: Outlook is the primary email client in Microsoft 365, available as a desktop application, web app (Outlook on the web), and mobile app. It connects to Exchange Online for email services.

questions.question: Which Microsoft 365 platform enables citizen developers to build custom business apps, automate workflows, and analyze data without writing code?
questions.explanation: Power Platform includes Power Apps (custom apps), Power Automate (workflow automation), Power BI (analytics), and Power Virtual Agents (chatbots) — enabling low-code/no-code development.

questions.question: Which Power Platform component allows users to build custom business applications with a drag-and-drop interface?
questions.explanation: Power Apps enables users to build custom business applications using a visual, drag-and-drop designer without writing traditional code. Apps can connect to Microsoft 365 data and other sources.

questions.question: Which Power Platform component automates repetitive tasks and business processes across applications?
questions.explanation: Power Automate (formerly Microsoft Flow) creates automated workflows between apps and services to synchronize files, get notifications, collect data, and automate business processes.

questions.question: Which Microsoft 365 suite is an employee experience platform that helps organizations improve engagement, learning, and well-being?
questions.explanation: Microsoft Viva is an employee experience platform with modules like Viva Insights (productivity and well-being), Viva Learning (training), Viva Connections (company communications), and Viva Topics (knowledge management).

questions.question: Which Microsoft Viva module provides personalized data-driven insights to help employees improve productivity and work-life balance?
questions.explanation: Viva Insights provides personalized, data-driven recommendations to help employees manage focus time, improve meeting habits, and maintain work-life balance based on Microsoft 365 usage patterns.

questions.question: Which Microsoft 365 feature allows users to co-author documents in real-time?
options: Co-authoring in Word, Excel, and PowerPoint
questions.explanation: Microsoft 365 apps (Word, Excel, PowerPoint) support real-time co-authoring, allowing multiple users to edit the same document simultaneously, whether online or in desktop apps.

questions.question: What is Windows 365?
options: A new version of the Windows operating system
A cloud PC service that streams a full Windows desktop from the cloud to any device
A mobile app for Windows phones
questions.explanation: Windows 365 is a cloud PC service that creates a full Windows desktop environment in the Microsoft Cloud and streams it to any device. Each user gets their own dedicated Cloud PC.

questions.question: What is Microsoft Loop?
options: A co-creation app with portable components that sync across Microsoft 365 apps
questions.explanation: Microsoft Loop is a co-creation experience with portable components that stay in sync across Teams, Outlook, Word, and other Microsoft 365 apps, enabling flexible real-time collaboration.

questions.question: Which Microsoft Teams feature allows external users from other organizations to join channels and access shared resources?
questions.explanation: Shared channels in Microsoft Teams allow users from external organizations to collaborate directly in a channel without switching tenants, using Microsoft Entra B2B direct connect.

questions.question: Which Microsoft 365 feature provides shared mailboxes for teams or departments that need a common email address?
questions.explanation: Shared mailboxes in Exchange Online provide a common email address (like info@company.com) that multiple users can access to read and send messages without requiring a separate license.

questions.question: Which Power Platform component enables organizations to build AI-powered chatbots without code?
questions.explanation: Power Virtual Agents (now part of Microsoft Copilot Studio) allows organizations to create AI-powered chatbots using a no-code graphical interface that can handle customer and employee inquiries.

questions.question: Which Microsoft 365 app is a task management tool integrated with Teams that uses boards, charts, and schedules?
questions.explanation: Microsoft Planner is a visual task management tool that helps teams organize work with boards, charts, and schedule views. It integrates directly with Microsoft Teams for seamless collaboration.

questions.question: Which Microsoft Viva module aggregates learning content from LinkedIn Learning, Microsoft Learn, and third-party providers into a single learning hub?
questions.explanation: Viva Learning integrates content from LinkedIn Learning, Microsoft Learn, and third-party learning management systems into a centralized hub within Microsoft Teams.

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of Microsoft 365 Groups?
options: They provide a single identity that gives a team access to a shared mailbox, calendar, SharePoint site, Planner, and OneNote
questions.explanation: Microsoft 365 Groups provide a unified membership model. Creating a group automatically provisions a shared mailbox, calendar, SharePoint site, Planner board, and OneNote notebook.

questions.question: In Microsoft Teams, what is the purpose of channels?
options: To organize conversations and files by topic, project, or department
questions.explanation: Channels in Microsoft Teams organize conversations, files, and tabs by topic, project, or department. Each team has a General channel and can have additional standard, private, or shared channels.

questions.question: How does Windows 365 differ from Azure Virtual Desktop?
options: Windows 365 is on-premises; Azure Virtual Desktop is cloud-based
Windows 365 provides a dedicated Cloud PC with fixed pricing per user; Azure Virtual Desktop provides pooled or personal desktops with consumption-based pricing
They are the same product with different names
Windows 365 only supports Linux; Azure Virtual Desktop supports Windows
questions.explanation: Windows 365 provides a dedicated Cloud PC per user with simple per-user-per-month pricing. Azure Virtual Desktop offers more flexibility with pooled/personal desktops and pay-as-you-go pricing.

questions.question: Which Power Platform component provides business intelligence and data visualization capabilities?
questions.explanation: Power BI is the business intelligence component of Power Platform, allowing users to connect to data sources, create interactive visualizations, and share dashboards and reports.

questions.question: Which Microsoft 365 feature enables organizations to build an intranet home site accessible from the Teams app bar?
questions.explanation: Viva Connections uses SharePoint to create a branded company intranet experience accessible directly from the Microsoft Teams app bar, providing news, resources, and personalized content.

questions.question: Which Microsoft 365 feature allows users to schedule and manage meetings with external participants who don't have a Microsoft account?
questions.explanation: Microsoft Bookings provides a web-based scheduling page where external customers and partners can book appointments without needing a Microsoft account, integrated with Exchange calendars.

questions.question: Which Microsoft Viva module provides goal-setting and OKR (Objectives and Key Results) tracking for organizations?
questions.explanation: Viva Goals is the OKR solution in the Viva suite. It helps organizations align daily work to strategic priorities through goal-setting, tracking progress, and connecting objectives to results.

questions.question: What is Microsoft Lists used for in Microsoft 365?
options: Tracking information with customizable lists for issues, assets, events, and more
questions.explanation: Microsoft Lists helps users track information and organize work with customizable, shareable lists. Templates include issue trackers, asset managers, event itineraries, and content schedulers.

questions.question: Which Microsoft 365 service provides enterprise video management and live streaming?
questions.explanation: Microsoft Stream is the enterprise video service in Microsoft 365 for uploading, managing, and sharing videos. It integrates with Teams, SharePoint, and other Microsoft 365 apps.

questions.question: What is Microsoft Copilot in Microsoft 365?
options: An AI assistant that uses large language models integrated into Microsoft 365 apps to help with writing, analysis, and productivity
A backup and disaster recovery service
questions.explanation: Microsoft Copilot in Microsoft 365 is an AI-powered assistant integrated into Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, and Teams. It uses large language models to help draft, summarize, analyze, and create content.

questions.question: Which Power Platform connectors allow Power Automate and Power Apps to interact with Microsoft 365 services and external systems?
questions.explanation: Power Platform uses Standard connectors (included with most licenses, e.g., SharePoint, Outlook) and Premium connectors (require additional licensing, e.g., SQL Server, Salesforce) to connect with services.

questions.question: Where are files stored when uploaded to a Microsoft Teams channel?
options: The team's SharePoint Online document library
questions.explanation: Files shared in a Teams channel are stored in the associated SharePoint Online team site's document library. Each channel has its own folder in the SharePoint library.

questions.question: Which Microsoft 365 application is used to create surveys, quizzes, and polls?
questions.explanation: Microsoft Forms is a tool for creating surveys, quizzes, and polls. It integrates with Excel for response analysis and can be embedded in Teams, SharePoint, and other apps.

questions.question: Which Microsoft Viva module provides a gateway to company news, conversations, and resources from within Teams?
questions.explanation: Viva Connections provides a personalized gateway in Microsoft Teams to company news, conversations, and resources built on SharePoint, creating a curated employee experience.

questions.question: Where are files stored when shared in a Microsoft Teams one-on-one or group chat (not a channel)?
questions.explanation: Files shared in Teams chats (not channels) are stored in the sender's OneDrive for Business. A 'Microsoft Teams Chat Files' folder is automatically created in the sender's OneDrive.

questions.question: Which component of Power Platform allows creating external-facing websites with business data?
questions.explanation: Power Pages (formerly Power Apps Portals) enables organizations to create external-facing websites where customers, partners, and employees can interact with business data securely.

questions.question: Which Microsoft service provides endpoint management for devices accessing Microsoft 365 resources?
questions.explanation: Microsoft Intune (part of Microsoft Endpoint Manager) provides mobile device management (MDM) and mobile application management (MAM) to secure and manage devices accessing Microsoft 365.

questions.question: What is the difference between SharePoint Online team sites and communication sites?
options: Team sites support file storage; communication sites do not
Team sites are for active collaboration among team members; communication sites are for broadcasting information to a wider audience
Communication sites require Azure AD Premium; team sites do not
questions.explanation: Team sites are designed for active collaboration within a team (connected to Microsoft 365 Groups). Communication sites are designed for broadcasting news and information to a broader audience.

questions.question: Which Microsoft 365 feature helps organizations manage and secure mobile devices and applications?
questions.explanation: Microsoft Intune provides mobile device management (MDM) and mobile application management (MAM), allowing organizations to control how devices access corporate data and enforce security policies.

questions.question: What is the Zero Trust security model?
options: Trust all users inside the corporate network
Never trust, always verify — treat every access request as if it comes from an untrusted network
Trust only users with admin privileges
Disable all external access to the network
questions.explanation: Zero Trust is a security model that assumes breach and verifies every access request regardless of location. Its principles are: verify explicitly, use least privilege access, and assume breach.

questions.question: Which Microsoft service provides identity and access management for Microsoft 365?
questions.explanation: Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is the cloud-based identity and access management service that authenticates users and controls access to Microsoft 365 and other cloud applications.

questions.question: What is Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA)?
options: Using multiple passwords for the same account
Requiring two or more verification methods (something you know, have, or are) to prove identity
questions.explanation: MFA requires users to provide two or more verification factors: something you know (password), something you have (phone/security key), or something you are (biometric). This greatly reduces account compromise.

questions.question: What is Conditional Access in Microsoft Entra ID?
options: A feature that automatically deletes inactive accounts
A policy engine that evaluates signals (user, device, location, risk) to grant, deny, or require additional verification for access
A VPN service for remote workers
questions.explanation: Conditional Access policies evaluate real-time signals (user identity, device compliance, location, application, risk level) to make automated access control decisions — allow, block, or require MFA.

questions.question: Which Microsoft security product provides endpoint detection and response (EDR) for devices?
questions.explanation: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint provides preventative protection, post-breach detection, automated investigation, and response for endpoints (Windows, macOS, Linux, iOS, Android devices).

questions.question: Which Microsoft Defender product protects against threats in email and collaboration tools like Teams and SharePoint?
questions.explanation: Microsoft Defender for Office 365 protects against advanced threats in email messages, links (URLs), attachments, and collaboration tools like Teams and SharePoint, including phishing and malware.

questions.question: Which Microsoft Defender product detects identity-based threats by monitoring Active Directory signals?
questions.explanation: Microsoft Defender for Identity monitors on-premises Active Directory signals to detect advanced threats, compromised identities, and malicious insider actions targeting your organization.

questions.question: What is Data Loss Prevention (DLP) in Microsoft 365?
options: A set of policies that prevent sensitive information from being shared or leaked outside the organization
questions.explanation: DLP policies in Microsoft 365 identify, monitor, and protect sensitive information (credit card numbers, SSNs, health records) from being accidentally or intentionally shared inappropriately.

questions.question: What are sensitivity labels in Microsoft 365?
options: Labels that mark the physical location of a document
Classifications applied to documents and emails that enforce protection like encryption and access restrictions
Tags used for search engine optimization
questions.explanation: Sensitivity labels classify and protect documents and emails by applying encryption, content markings (headers/footers/watermarks), and access restrictions based on the label's protection settings.

questions.question: Which Microsoft solution provides a unified compliance portal for managing compliance, governance, and risk across Microsoft 365?
questions.explanation: Microsoft Purview (formerly Microsoft 365 Compliance) provides a unified portal for information protection, data governance, risk management, compliance assessment, and eDiscovery.

questions.question: What is Microsoft Purview Compliance Manager?
options: A tool that assesses an organization's compliance posture and provides recommendations to meet regulatory requirements
questions.explanation: Compliance Manager provides a compliance score, pre-built assessments for regulations (GDPR, HIPAA, ISO 27001), and improvement actions to help organizations manage and improve their compliance posture.

questions.question: Which three principles form the foundation of the Zero Trust security model?
options: Encrypt everything, block all traffic, audit annually
Verify explicitly, use least privilege access, assume breach
Trust internal users, block external users, encrypt at rest
Monitor logs, patch weekly, backup daily
questions.explanation: Zero Trust is built on three principles: (1) Verify explicitly — always authenticate and authorize based on all available data points. (2) Use least privilege access. (3) Assume breach — minimize blast radius.

questions.question: What does Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps provide?
options: A Cloud Access Security Broker (CASB) that monitors and controls cloud app usage
questions.explanation: Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps is a CASB (Cloud Access Security Broker) that provides visibility, data control, and threat protection across cloud services, detecting shadow IT and risky app usage.

questions.question: What is the purpose of Microsoft Purview Information Barriers?
options: To prevent data from leaving the country
To restrict communication and collaboration between specific groups of users to prevent conflicts of interest
questions.explanation: Information Barriers create policies that prevent specific groups from communicating with each other in Teams, SharePoint, and OneDrive, addressing conflict-of-interest and regulatory requirements.

questions.question: What is eDiscovery in Microsoft 365?
options: A tool for discovering new cloud services
A process and set of tools for identifying, collecting, and producing electronically stored information for legal cases
A user discovery feature for Active Directory
questions.explanation: eDiscovery in Microsoft 365 enables organizations to search, hold, and export content from Exchange, SharePoint, OneDrive, and Teams for legal investigations and compliance requirements.

questions.question: Which authentication method in Microsoft Entra ID eliminates the need for passwords?
options: Passwordless authentication (FIDO2 keys, Windows Hello, Authenticator app)
questions.explanation: Passwordless authentication methods like FIDO2 security keys, Windows Hello for Business, and the Microsoft Authenticator app eliminate passwords, reducing phishing risk and improving user experience.

questions.question: What does Microsoft Purview Data Lifecycle Management help organizations do?
options: Retain or delete content based on retention policies to meet compliance obligations
questions.explanation: Data Lifecycle Management uses retention policies and retention labels to keep content for required periods and delete it when no longer needed, helping meet regulatory and compliance obligations.

questions.question: What is Microsoft 365 Defender?
options: A unified pre- and post-breach enterprise defense suite that coordinates detection, prevention, investigation, and response across endpoints, identities, email, and applications
An antivirus scanner for Windows only
questions.explanation: Microsoft 365 Defender is an integrated XDR (Extended Detection and Response) solution that correlates signals across Defender for Endpoint, Identity, Office 365, and Cloud Apps for unified threat management.

questions.question: What is the purpose of Microsoft Entra Privileged Identity Management (PIM)?
options: To permanently assign admin roles to all users
To provide just-in-time, time-bound access to privileged roles to reduce the risk of excessive permissions
To disable MFA for admin accounts
questions.explanation: PIM provides just-in-time privileged access, time-bound role assignments, approval workflows, and audit trails for privileged roles, reducing the attack surface of standing admin permissions.

questions.question: Where can organizations find Microsoft's privacy commitments, data handling practices, and compliance certifications?
questions.explanation: The Microsoft Trust Center provides information about Microsoft's security, privacy, compliance, and transparency practices, including audit reports, certifications, and data processing details.

questions.question: What is the purpose of retention policies in Microsoft 365?
options: To specify how long content should be retained and when it should be deleted to meet regulatory requirements
questions.explanation: Retention policies define how long content in Exchange, SharePoint, OneDrive, Teams, and other services should be retained before deletion, helping organizations meet regulatory and legal requirements.

questions.question: What does Exchange Online Protection (EOP) provide?
options: Cloud-based email filtering to protect against spam, malware, and phishing
questions.explanation: Exchange Online Protection (EOP) is the cloud-based filtering service that protects against spam, malware, and known phishing in inbound and outbound email messages.

questions.question: Which Microsoft Entra feature evaluates real-time sign-in risk and can automatically require MFA or block access when suspicious activity is detected?
questions.explanation: Microsoft Entra ID Protection uses machine learning to detect and respond to identity-based risks. It evaluates sign-in risk and user risk in real-time, triggering automated remediation like MFA or blocking.

questions.question: What is Microsoft Purview Insider Risk Management?
options: A tool for managing external vendor risk
A solution that detects and helps mitigate internal risks like data theft, data leaks, and policy violations by analyzing user activities
A physical building access control system
questions.explanation: Insider Risk Management uses signals from Microsoft 365 and other sources to detect potentially risky user activities (data exfiltration, security violations) and helps security teams investigate and act.

questions.question: Which Microsoft 365 feature allows administrators to apply encryption to emails so only authorized recipients can read them?
questions.explanation: Microsoft Purview Message Encryption allows organizations to send encrypted email to anyone inside or outside the organization, ensuring only authorized recipients can read the message content.

questions.question: What is the purpose of Microsoft Entra Connect?
options: To connect Azure VMs to on-premises networks
To synchronize on-premises Active Directory identities to Microsoft Entra ID for hybrid identity
questions.explanation: Microsoft Entra Connect synchronizes user identities between on-premises Active Directory and Microsoft Entra ID, enabling users to sign in to both on-premises and cloud services with one identity.

questions.question: Which Microsoft security tool provides SIEM (Security Information and Event Management) and SOAR (Security Orchestration, Automation, and Response) capabilities?
questions.explanation: Microsoft Sentinel is a cloud-native SIEM and SOAR solution that collects security data at scale, detects threats using AI, and enables automated response through playbooks and analytics rules.

questions.question: What is Microsoft Purview Communication Compliance?
options: A tool for managing phone system quality
A solution that detects policy violations in communications such as harassment, inappropriate content, or regulatory violations
questions.explanation: Communication Compliance uses policies to scan emails, Teams messages, and other communications for regulatory violations, harassment, threats, and inappropriate content, enabling investigation and remediation.

questions.question: What is the purpose of Microsoft Purview Data Loss Prevention for endpoints?
options: To monitor and restrict sensitive data activities on Windows and macOS devices, such as copying to USB drives or uploading to unauthorized cloud services
To encrypt the entire hard drive
questions.explanation: Endpoint DLP extends data loss prevention to Windows and macOS devices, monitoring and restricting activities like copying sensitive files to USB drives, printing, or uploading to unauthorized cloud services.

questions.question: Which security feature requires users to register a secondary verification method (like a phone number) in addition to their password?
questions.explanation: Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) requires an additional verification method beyond the password, such as a phone call, text message, authenticator app, or biometric scan.

questions.question: What is Safe Attachments in Microsoft Defender for Office 365?
options: A feature that compresses email attachments
A feature that opens attachments in a virtual sandbox to detect malware before delivering them to users
A feature that blocks all attachments
A feature that converts attachments to PDF
questions.explanation: Safe Attachments opens email attachments in a virtual sandbox environment to detect malicious content before delivering the email. If malware is found, the attachment is blocked or removed.

questions.question: What is Safe Links in Microsoft Defender for Office 365?
options: A feature that shortens URLs in emails
A feature that checks URLs at time of click to protect against malicious websites
A feature that removes all hyperlinks from emails
questions.explanation: Safe Links provides time-of-click verification of URLs in email messages and documents. When a user clicks a link, it is checked against a list of known malicious sites and blocked if necessary.

questions.question: What is Microsoft Purview Audit used for?
options: Logging and searching user and admin activities across Microsoft 365 services for security investigation and compliance
questions.explanation: Microsoft Purview Audit captures and logs user and admin activities across Microsoft 365 services, enabling organizations to search audit logs for security investigations, compliance, and forensic analysis.

questions.question: What is Single Sign-On (SSO) in the context of Microsoft Entra ID?
options: Using one password for all personal accounts
A feature that allows users to authenticate once and access multiple applications without re-entering credentials
questions.explanation: SSO allows users to sign in once to Microsoft Entra ID and then access all connected applications (Microsoft 365, SaaS apps, on-premises apps) without additional authentication prompts.

questions.question: What does Microsoft Purview Information Protection help organizations do?
options: Discover, classify, and protect sensitive information wherever it lives or travels
questions.explanation: Information Protection helps organizations discover and classify sensitive data, apply protection (encryption, access restrictions, visual markings) through sensitivity labels, and track protected content.

questions.question: What is the Microsoft Service Trust Portal?
options: A marketplace for purchasing Microsoft services
A portal providing audit reports, compliance guides, and trust documents about Microsoft cloud services
A help desk for technical support
A tool for deploying Azure resources
questions.explanation: The Service Trust Portal provides third-party audit reports (SOC, ISO, FedRAMP), compliance guides, privacy documentation, and other trust documents for Microsoft cloud services.

questions.question: What is role-based access control (RBAC) in Microsoft 365?
options: Granting all users full administrative access
Assigning permissions to users based on their job function/role, following the principle of least privilege
A physical access control system for offices
questions.explanation: RBAC assigns permissions based on organizational roles (Global Admin, User Admin, etc.) rather than to individual users, following the least privilege principle to reduce security risk.

questions.question: What is the purpose of Microsoft Purview Data Map?
options: Creating geographic maps of office locations
Automatically scanning and classifying data across multi-cloud and on-premises environments to create a unified data governance map
questions.explanation: Microsoft Purview Data Map automatically scans and classifies data assets across Azure, AWS, GCP, and on-premises environments, creating a unified map for data governance, discovery, and lineage tracking.

questions.question: Which Microsoft 365 plan is designed for enterprise organizations and includes advanced security and compliance features?
questions.explanation: Microsoft 365 E5 is the most comprehensive enterprise plan, including advanced security (Defender for Office 365 Plan 2), compliance (eDiscovery Premium), analytics (Power BI Pro), and audio conferencing.

questions.question: Which Microsoft 365 plan tier is specifically designed for frontline workers?
questions.explanation: Microsoft 365 F1 and F3 plans are designed for frontline workers who primarily use shared devices and need basic productivity tools, communication, and identity management.

questions.question: What is the primary difference between Microsoft 365 E3 and E5?
options: E5 includes Word and Excel; E3 does not
E5 includes advanced security, compliance, analytics, and voice capabilities not available in E3
E3 supports more users than E5
There is no difference; they are the same plan
questions.explanation: E5 builds on E3 by adding advanced security (Defender P2, Cloud App Security), advanced compliance (eDiscovery Premium, Insider Risk), Power BI Pro, and Phone System/Audio Conferencing.

questions.question: Which Microsoft 365 plan tier is designed for small and medium businesses with up to 300 users?
questions.explanation: Microsoft 365 Business Basic, Business Standard, and Business Premium are designed for small and medium businesses with up to 300 users, offering different feature tiers at lower price points.

questions.question: How are Microsoft 365 subscriptions typically billed?
questions.explanation: Microsoft 365 uses a per-user, per-month subscription model (billed monthly or annually). Each licensed user gets access to the services included in their plan.

questions.question: What is the difference between monthly and annual Microsoft 365 subscription billing?
options: Annual billing provides different features than monthly
Annual commitment typically costs less per month than month-to-month billing
Monthly billing is only available for E5 plans
There is no price difference between the two
questions.explanation: Annual commitment subscriptions typically offer a lower per-user, per-month price compared to month-to-month billing. Both provide the same features within the same plan tier.

questions.question: An organization with 500 users needs desktop Office apps, email, Teams, and advanced threat protection but does NOT need phone system or eDiscovery Premium. Which plan is the best fit?
options: Microsoft 365 E3 with Defender for Office 365 add-on
questions.explanation: E3 provides desktop Office apps, Exchange, Teams, and core security. Adding Defender for Office 365 as an add-on provides advanced threat protection without paying for E5 features they do not need.

questions.question: What is the Microsoft 365 admin center used for?
options: Managing users, licenses, billing, and service settings for Microsoft 365
questions.explanation: The Microsoft 365 admin center (admin.microsoft.com) is the centralized portal where administrators manage users, groups, licenses, billing, service health, and organization settings.

questions.question: Where can administrators view the current health status of Microsoft 365 services?
options: Service health dashboard in the Microsoft 365 admin center
questions.explanation: The Service health dashboard in the Microsoft 365 admin center shows the current status of all Microsoft 365 services, active incidents, planned maintenance, and advisory notices.

questions.question: What is a Service Level Agreement (SLA) in the context of Microsoft 365?
options: A commitment by Microsoft to maintain a specific level of service uptime and performance
questions.explanation: An SLA is a contractual commitment from Microsoft guaranteeing a specific level of service availability (typically 99.9% uptime). If the SLA is not met, customers may be eligible for service credits.

questions.question: What is the financially backed uptime SLA that Microsoft provides for most Microsoft 365 services?
questions.explanation: Microsoft provides a financially backed 99.9% uptime SLA for most Microsoft 365 services. If uptime falls below this threshold, customers may receive service credits as compensation.

questions.question: What happens if Microsoft does not meet the uptime SLA for a Microsoft 365 service?
options: Affected customers may be eligible for service credits applied to their bill
Microsoft automatically refunds the full subscription cost
questions.explanation: If Microsoft fails to meet the SLA, affected customers can submit a claim and may receive service credits (a percentage discount on their next bill) based on the level of downtime experienced.

questions.question: Where does an administrator go to add or remove user licenses in Microsoft 365?
questions.explanation: Administrators manage user licenses (assign, remove, change plans) in the Microsoft 365 admin center under the Users section or the Billing > Licenses page.

questions.question: Which Microsoft support option provides the fastest response time for critical issues with 24/7 access to Microsoft engineers?
questions.explanation: Microsoft Unified Support provides the highest level of enterprise support with 24/7 access to Microsoft engineers, fastest response times for critical issues, and dedicated account management.

questions.question: What are add-on licenses in Microsoft 365?
options: Licenses that replace the base subscription
Additional licenses that extend the capabilities of a base subscription with specific features
questions.explanation: Add-on licenses provide additional capabilities on top of a base Microsoft 365 subscription, such as Defender for Office 365, Phone System, Audio Conferencing, or extra storage.

questions.question: Which Microsoft 365 Business plan includes advanced security features like Defender for Office 365 and Intune?
questions.explanation: Microsoft 365 Business Premium includes everything in Business Standard plus advanced security features including Defender for Office 365 Plan 1, Intune, Conditional Access, and Azure Information Protection.

questions.question: What is Microsoft FastTrack?
options: A performance optimization tool for Office apps
A free deployment and adoption service that helps organizations plan, onboard, and drive usage of Microsoft 365
A fast internet service from Microsoft
questions.explanation: Microsoft FastTrack is a free benefit for customers with 150+ licenses that provides planning, onboarding, migration, and adoption guidance to help organizations successfully deploy Microsoft 365.

questions.question: What is Microsoft 365 Apps for Enterprise (formerly Office 365 ProPlus)?
options: A cloud-only service with no desktop apps
A subscription that provides the full desktop Office applications (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, etc.)
questions.explanation: Microsoft 365 Apps for Enterprise provides the fully installed desktop versions of Office applications including Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, OneNote, Access, and Publisher (Windows only).

questions.question: Which Microsoft 365 plan includes only web and mobile versions of Office apps (no desktop apps) along with email and Teams?
questions.explanation: Microsoft 365 Business Basic includes web and mobile versions of Office apps, Exchange Online email, Teams, SharePoint, and OneDrive — but does NOT include desktop Office application installs.

questions.question: Which tool in the Microsoft 365 admin center helps administrators manage message flow, spam filtering, and email policies?
questions.explanation: The Exchange admin center (accessible from the Microsoft 365 admin center) is where administrators manage mail flow rules, spam/phishing policies, connectors, and email organization settings.

questions.question: How can administrators submit a support request for Microsoft 365?
options: Through the Microsoft 365 admin center using the 'Help & support' option
Only by email to a generic address
questions.explanation: Administrators can submit support requests directly from the Microsoft 365 admin center using the Help & support option, which allows them to describe the issue and receive assistance.

questions.question: What is the difference between Microsoft 365 and Office 365?
options: Microsoft 365 includes Office 365 apps PLUS Windows, security, and device management; Office 365 was a standalone productivity suite
Microsoft 365 is free; Office 365 is paid
questions.explanation: Microsoft 365 bundles Office 365 productivity apps with Windows 10/11 Enterprise licensing, Enterprise Mobility + Security (EMS), and device management. Office 365 was the standalone cloud productivity suite.

questions.question: What is the Message Center in the Microsoft 365 admin center?
options: A tool for sending emails to users
A communication hub where Microsoft posts upcoming changes, new features, and required admin actions
questions.explanation: The Message Center in the admin center is where Microsoft communicates upcoming changes, new features, deprecations, and required admin actions. Admins should review it regularly.

questions.question: Which Microsoft 365 subscription model allows organizations to pay only for what they use with no long-term commitment?
questions.explanation: Month-to-month subscriptions allow organizations to pay per user per month without a long-term commitment, providing flexibility to add or remove users as needed.

questions.question: Which support option is included at no additional cost with every Microsoft 365 subscription?
options: 24/7 phone and web support for critical issues via the admin center
questions.explanation: All Microsoft 365 subscriptions include basic support with 24/7 phone and web access for critical (severity A) issues through the admin center, plus business hours support for non-critical issues.

questions.question: An organization wants to provide Office desktop apps to users but does not need email or collaboration services. Which plan should they choose?
questions.explanation: Microsoft 365 Apps for Business provides the desktop Office applications (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, etc.) and OneDrive without the collaboration services like Exchange, Teams, or SharePoint.

questions.question: Which Microsoft 365 admin center feature allows administrators to simulate security attacks to test user awareness?
questions.explanation: Attack simulation training (in the Microsoft Defender portal, accessible from admin center) allows admins to run simulated phishing, password spray, and other attacks to test and train users.

questions.question: A university needs to provide Microsoft 365 to 50,000 students and staff at a discounted rate. Which licensing approach is most appropriate?
options: Microsoft 365 Education plans (A1, A3, A5) through academic volume licensing
Microsoft 365 Business Basic for each user
questions.explanation: Microsoft 365 Education plans (A1 free, A3, A5) are specifically designed for qualified academic institutions, offering discounted or free licensing for students and faculty through volume licensing.

questions.question: Which report in the Microsoft 365 admin center shows how users are adopting and using Microsoft 365 services?
questions.explanation: Usage reports in the Microsoft 365 admin center show adoption metrics for Teams, OneDrive, SharePoint, Exchange, and other services, helping administrators understand how the organization uses Microsoft 365.

questions.question: What is the difference between Microsoft Unified Support and standard support?
options: Unified Support provides a dedicated account team, proactive services, and faster response times for an additional fee
Standard support includes on-site visits; Unified does not
Unified Support is only available for Azure, not Microsoft 365
questions.explanation: Microsoft Unified Support (paid, premium tier) provides a dedicated Customer Success Account Manager, proactive services, faster response times, and support across all Microsoft products under one agreement.

questions.question: Can Microsoft 365 licenses be transferred between users?
options: No, licenses are permanently tied to the first assigned user
Yes, administrators can unassign a license from one user and reassign it to another
Only if both users are in the same department
Only Microsoft support can transfer licenses
questions.explanation: Microsoft 365 licenses are per-user but not permanently tied to one user. Administrators can unassign a license from a departing user and reassign it to a new user through the admin center.

questions.question: Which Microsoft 365 admin center feature allows administrators to configure organization-wide settings like password policies and theme?
options: Settings > Org settings in the admin center
questions.explanation: The Settings > Org settings section in the Microsoft 365 admin center allows administrators to configure organization-wide settings including password policies, themes, release preferences, and more.

questions.question: Which Microsoft 365 plan provides desktop Office apps, email, Teams, and basic security for small businesses?
questions.explanation: Microsoft 365 Business Standard includes desktop Office apps, Exchange Online, Teams, SharePoint, and OneDrive. It provides a good balance of productivity and collaboration for small businesses.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the Microsoft 365 Adoption Score (previously Productivity Score)?
options: To provide insights into how the organization uses Microsoft 365 and recommend improvements for technology experience and adoption
questions.explanation: Adoption Score provides organizational-level insights into Microsoft 365 usage across communication, collaboration, content management, and teamwork, with recommendations to improve adoption.

questions.question: A company has 200 employees: 150 office workers who need full desktop apps and 50 retail floor workers who only need Teams and a shared device experience. What is the most cost-effective licensing approach?
options: Microsoft 365 E3 for 150 office workers and Microsoft 365 F3 for 50 frontline workers
Microsoft 365 Business Basic for all 200 users
Microsoft 365 Business Premium for all 200 users
questions.explanation: Using E3 for office workers who need full desktop apps and F3 for frontline workers who only need Teams and basic tools is the most cost-effective mix-and-match licensing approach.

questions.question: Which feature in the Microsoft 365 admin center allows administrators to control whether their organization receives new features early or on the standard release schedule?
questions.explanation: Release preferences allow admins to choose between Targeted release (early access to new features for the entire org or specific users) and Standard release (features after they are broadly available).

questions.question: What are service credits in the context of Microsoft 365 SLAs?
options: Monetary credits applied to a customer's bill when Microsoft fails to meet guaranteed uptime levels
questions.explanation: Service credits are monetary credits applied to a customer's Microsoft 365 bill when Microsoft's actual service uptime falls below the guaranteed SLA percentage. Customers must file a claim to receive them.
